issue_title: When the Delete Credential required action is set to false an authentication application cannot be removed from the account UI
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area account/ui ### Describe the bug When the required action Delete Credential is set to false in the UI at /admin/master/console/#/master/authentication/required-actions the Delete button in the UI at /realms/master/account/#/account-security/signing-in stops working. You can click on it and the page refreshes, but the device is not removed. Note that despite the migration code for 24.0.3 setting the new setting to true [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/3e243d77ec84663c930301b2621aa311860a4f2b/model/storage-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/migration/migrators/MigrateTo24_0_3.java#L54) and [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/24.0.3/server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/utils/DefaultRequiredActions.java#L216), the setting Delete Credential was set to false for us after a migration from 23.0.0. This means that after migration the deletion of the device from the account UI stopped working. ### Version 24.0.5 ### Regression - [ ] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior The button works and the page navigates to /realms/master/login-actions/required-action?execution=delete_credential&client_id=account-console&tab_id=kXy2mtc9h1A ### Actual behavior The page refreshes, but device is not removed. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Migrate to version 24.0.5 from a version before 24.0.3 OR start Keycloak using ```shell docker run -p 8081:8080 \ -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN=admin -e KEYCLOAK_ADMIN_PASSWORD=admin \ quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:24.0.5 start-dev ``` and login with user admin with password admin and navigate to /admin/master/console/#/master/authentication/required-actions and set Disable Credential to Off. 1. Navigate to the account page at /realms/master/account/#/account-security/signing-in 1. Set up an Authenticator application 1. Click Delete, and the page will refresh, but not remove the device. ### Anything else? _No response_
commit_message:
Hide the 'Delete' button in the account console when DeleteCredentialAction is disabled or unavailable
closes #30204

issue_title: Base theme contains properties without default values
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

Some resources of the `base` theme contain properites-placeholders for CSS classnames, which doesn't have defaults:
`${properties.kcClassNameAttribute}` instead of `${properties.kcClassNameAttribute!}` (the missing `!`)

This yields into ugly Freemarker errors/exceptions if one uses a custom theme inheriting from `base` and the properties are not set.

### Version

24.x, nightly

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The placeholders use empty default values to prevent Freemarker exceptions.

### Actual behavior

Freemarker errors/exceptions are being thrown if one uses a custom theme inheriting from `base` and the properties are not set. Error page in browser is shown.

### How to Reproduce?

create custom theme, inherit from `base`, don't set any properties for css classnames, use e.g. the `recovery-authn-code` templates...

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Custom attributes are removed during UPDATE PROFILE event
issue_body: ### Before reporting an issue - [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them. ### Area core ### Describe the bug We have a custom User Storage Provider SPI which syncs users from an external system. We're using a custom attribute to reference the user across both systems. After upgrading from Keycloak v22 to v24 we noticed a very small number of users were suddenly missing their essential attributes. After a lot of debugging I came across these events which were triggered for exactly those few users that are now missing their attributes: After the Keycloak upgrade, the "Unmanaged Attributes" option in the realm was correctly set to "Enabled" to preserve the pre-v24 behaviour. I'm not sure what even causes the "UPDATE PROFILE" event in the first place because we're not promoting the Account Console to our users (there are only 15 UPDATE PROFILE events in the last few months, so fortunately it's rather rare). Nonetheless, custom attributes getting deleted on profile updates is quite serious. I suspect it has got something to do with the new User Profile feature that was enabled by default in Keycloak v24, but I think it's supposed to behave exactly like previous versions when doing an upgrade, isn't it? We have those custom attributes write-protected by listing them in the `KC_SPI_USER_PROFILE_DECLARATIVE_USER_PROFILE_READ_ONLY_ATTRIBUTES` and `KC_SPI_USER_PROFILE_DECLARATIVE_USER_PROFILE_ADMIN_READ_ONLY_ATTRIBUTES` environment variables and this seems to be the key: Every attribute listed here is getting removed when updating your profile. Any ideas would be greatly appreciated! ### Version 24.0.3 ### Regression - [X] The issue is a regression ### Expected behavior Custom attributes should be preserved when profile is updated. ### Actual behavior Custom attributes are getting removed when profile is updated. ### How to Reproduce? 1. Using the Keycloak UI, set the attribute `MY_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE` to some value. 2. Protect this custom attribute by setting `KC_SPI_USER_PROFILE_DECLARATIVE_USER_PROFILE_READ_ONLY_ATTRIBUTES=MY_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE` and restart Keycloak. 3. Go to the account console and update your profile. 4. `MY_CUSTOM_ATTRIBUTE` will be gone. ### Anything else? _No response_

issue_title: Executor consent-required does not work for client-roles condition
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oidc

### Describe the bug

It seems that client-roles condition is not working for consent-required executor.



### Version

24.0.4

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When client-role condition is set for a client role, consent-required executor sets "Consent required" field of  matched clients  as "ON" in updating configuration of clients. 

### Actual behavior

"Consent required" field of of a client is not set "ON" in updating configuration of the client, where client role matches the role in "client-role".

### How to Reproduce?

1) In realm settings -> Client policies, create a profile containing "consent-required" executor
2) Create a policy, condition is set as "client-roles (client roles is "testrole"), profile is set as the profile created in 1)
3) Create a OIDC client "testclient"
4) In Roles tab of  "testclient", create role "testrole"
5) For "testclient" set consent required "ON" and save
6) For "testclient" set consent required "OFF" and save
7) See "testclient" again, I can see consent required is still "OFF", but "ON" is expected, because "consent-required" executor was set in 1)-4).

### Anything else?

When I set other executors like "secure-session" with "client-roles" condition, it was applied to "testclient".

issue_title: Upgrade to Keycloak 25 - Table 'USER_CONSENT' is specified twice on MySQL/MariaDB database
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When migrating Keycloak from 24.0.x to 25.0.0 running on database MySQL 8 or MariaDB 10.2 the migration `unique-consentuser` fails due to 

`liquibase.exception.LiquibaseException: liquibase.exception.MigrationFailedException: Migration failed for changeset META-INF/jpa-changelog-25.0.0.xml::unique-consentuser::keycloak:
keycloak-1  |      Reason: liquibase.exception.DatabaseException: (conn=2816) Table 'USER_CONSENT' is specified twice, both as a target for 'DELETE' and as a separate source for data [Failed SQL: (1093) DELETE FROM keycloak.USER_CONSENT WHERE ID IN ( SELECT uc.ID FROM keycloak.USER_CONSENT uc INNER JOIN ( SELECT CLIENT_ID, USER_ID, MAX(LAST_UPDATED_DATE) AS MAX_UPDATED_DATE FROM keycloak.USER_CONSENT GROUP BY CLIENT_ID, USER_ID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ) max_dates ON uc.CLIENT_ID = max_dates.CLIENT_ID AND uc.USER_ID = max_dates.USER_ID AND uc.LAST_UPDATED_DATE = max_dates.MAX_UPDATED_DATE )]`

The SQL is not ok for MySQL/MariaDB

    DELETE FROM keycloak.USER_CONSENT WHERE ID IN ( SELECT uc.ID FROM keycloak.USER_CONSENT uc INNER JOIN ( SELECT CLIENT_ID, USER_ID, MAX(LAST_UPDATED_DATE) AS MAX_UPDATED_DATE FROM keycloak.USER_CONSENT GROUP BY CLIENT_ID, USER_ID HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ) max_dates ON uc.CLIENT_ID = max_dates.CLIENT_ID AND uc.USER_ID = max_dates.USER_ID AND uc.LAST_UPDATED_DATE = max_dates.MAX_UPDATED_DATE )

since not allowed as per https://mariadb.com/kb/en/e1093/

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Upgrade to 25.0.0 being completed successfully

### Actual behavior

Upgrade to 25.0.0 fails

### How to Reproduce?

Just start a Keycloak 24.x.x running on MySQL/MariaDB db and upgrade to Keycloak 25.0.0

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

With #27656 the `do...()` methods of `SimpleHttp` were refactored by @ahus1 . I generally welcome this change, but...

The `do..()` methods with the `KeycloakSession` as second parameter are still public, the ones with the `HttpClient` are now protected. 
And there are no public `doPut()`, `doPatch()`, and `doHead()` methods with arguments `(String url, KeycloakSession session)`. Before the change, we could use the methods with the `HttpClient` for these methods. Now this is no more possible when using `SimpleHttp` as a good and simple http client inside of Keycloak extensions.
The use of `SimpleHttp` is the only easy, simple method to use a http client, as Keycloak doesn't provide other easy possibilities. Or does is meanwhile?

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Provide public static `SimpleHttp.do...()` methods for all http methods by default.

### Actual behavior

Only a few http methods are reflected by the public `SimpleHttp.do...()` methods.

### How to Reproduce?

Try to use the `SimpleHttp` class in a custom extension and try to use the `SimpleHttp.doPut(...)` method.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Identity-first login flow should be followed by asking for the user credentials rather than allowing providing the username again
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

organizations

### Describe the bug

When running the identity-first login flow and once providing an email address, if an account already exists the user is redirected to the regular login page to continue the authentication by providing the credentials.

However, the second-step is still showing the username field and that does not make much sense given that the user already identified him/herself.

In Keycloak, we have a specific flow to only ask the user for credentials once the user is identified. For that, we need to change the `OrganizationAuthenticator` to set the user to the authentication context so that the next flow will only ask for the credentials.

Whether or not this make sense all the time or if we want a specific setting to decide on the expected behavior, we can discuss.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

After running the identity-first login, do not ask for a username/email again.

### Actual behavior

The username/email field is shown again after running the identity-first login.

### How to Reproduce?

Try to authenticate to an org-aware realm using an account that already exists.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Make sure it is not possible to run snapshot server against production DB by default
issue_body:
## Description

Make sure that it is not possible to run snapshot server against production DB as this is pretty much the invalid situation. When people try to use production DB against snapshot server ( server version like `999.0.0`  ), it signals the situation, which is very likely incorrect. It can lead to the broken/inconsistent data in the database and the issues like the one described below.

## Original title

Check for 999.0.0 in MIGRATION_MODEL breaks Keycloak instances without documented workaround



### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

~~operator~~ core (edited by @mabartos )

### Describe the bug

Feel free to move this to a discussion.

After updating Keycloak to version 25.0.0 one of our Keycloak instances failed to start with the following error message:

```
Incorrect state of migration. You are trying to run server version '25.0.0' against a database which was migrated to snapshot version '999.0.0'. Databases that have been migrated to a snapshot version can't be migrated to a released version of Keycloak or to a more recent snapshot version.
```

That check makes sense and there was an advisory in the Keycloak 24 Update notes regarding the operator accidentally deploying a snapshot. We never hit that issue (knowingly), but we must have mistakenly run a snapshot in an update a few versions ago already:

```
select * from migration_model ;
  id   | version | update_time 
-------+---------+-------------
 33jeh | 21.0.1  |  ...
 84vyx | 21.0.2  |  ...
 uz6gn | 21.1.0  |  ...
 37dtv | 21.1.1  |  ...
 jxdii | 21.1.2  |  ...
 e4cpa | 22.0.0  |  ...
 nmk0o | 22.0.1  |  ...
 r21q6 | 22.0.2  |  ...
 gn2wq | 22.0.3  |  ...
 2mtf8 | 999.0.0 |  ...
```

Needless to say that the database update we took prior to the upgrade wasn't of much help and we didn't notice the problem because Keycloak never complained about this during the last updates and there were no operational problems.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The error message helps the user how to trigger the correct migrations and a note of that "breaking change" is in the release notes.

### Actual behavior

The error message is hard to search and no official guidance on how to get out of this is provided. Especially when the accidental run of the snapshot could already be quite some time in the past.

### How to Reproduce?

* Run a nightly.
* Run Keycloak 25.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Attempt to run snapshot Keycloak server against production DB should fail during migration

closes #30364

Signed-off-by: mposolda <mposolda@gmail.com>
Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Login / Admin events filter by date under realm Events return incorrect results
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Using the `Date (to)` and `Date (from)` filter in User Events / Admin Events will return the incorrect result. Both start and end dates use the 00:00:00 time for the dates, which is fine for the start date, but wrong for the end date. If one sets the same date in both fields, it returns no events when it should return all events from that date. The end date, or `Date (to)`, should be using 23:59:59 as the time so it includes all events from that day.

A workaround exists, which is to set the end date to the following day, but the user experience is currently not the best.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When filtering events, the events from the end date (`Date (to)`) should be visible.

### Actual behavior

When filtering events, the events from the end date (`Date (to)`) are not displayed.

### How to Reproduce?

Enable events in the realm, generate some events by logging in and out, then check the events screen. Using the date filter with the same date in `Date (from)` and `Date (to)` returns no results.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Client Roles are not shown when clientId property is set
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

token-exchange

### Describe the bug

When defining a client scope for role mapping and setting the client id, there are no roles shown in the token.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Only roles of the specified client id will be shown

### Actual behavior

no roles are shown

### How to Reproduce?

* Create a new client (eg: `test`)
* Add a new role to the created client like `ABC`
* Go to any existing user (eg. admin) and assign it the newly created client role `ABC`:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/13119203/5663fae5-cf89-4a69-b6e1-a90a469cc962)
* go back to the created client, navigate to the client scopes and selected the dedicated one (first one)
* Add a predefined mapper and select `client roles` and click add
* Select the added mapper and change the `Token claim name` to something like `test_roles`
* Go back to the client scopes tab of the added client and switch to `Evaluate` nested tab
* select the user (eg `Admin`) which you previously assigned to role (eg `ABC`) to and click on `Generated access token` to see an example of how an example token looks like. In the output you should then see something like:
```
  "test_roles": [
    "ABC",
    ....
    ....
  ]
```
* now switch back to client scope setup and select the dedicated one
* Click the previously added `client roles` mapper 
* Set the `client id` property to the clients name (eg `test`)
It should now look like:
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/13119203/2c2b8b86-eaca-4faf-858a-e364a15785b6)
* Click save
* Go back to the client scopes tab of the added client and switch to `Evaluate` nested tab
* select the user (eg `Admin`) which you previously assigned to role (eg `ABC`) to and click on `Generated access token` to see an example of how an example token looks like.
* Now the `test_roles` will no longer be shown, even if the created `ABC` role does match to the client that is used in the role mapper client id.

issue_title: Migration stuck if versions incompatible
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

When performing a keycloak upgrade to a version that has an incompatible infinispan version keycloak is stuck with one of three pods running (on the old version) and with another one (on the new version) being unable to get healthy.


### Version

24.05

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When performing a keycloak upgrade, the operator is supposed to scale down the keycloak statefulset to one pod (on the new version) to make keycloak perform a database migration on just one pod instead of having multiple pods running. After the pod is back up and healthy the statefulset is scaled back up to three pods.

The described process would make all pods go away and start a new pod on the new version, do the possibly needed migration, and then start another one until all three are running again.

### Actual behavior

When performing a keycloak upgrade, the operator scales down the keycloak statefulset to one pod (on the old version) as soon as this is done it updates the version in the statefulset to the new one and scales it back to three pods.

### How to Reproduce?

Do an upgrade from keycloak v23 to v24 using the operator by changing the image in the keycloak resource.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
fix(operator): Scale statefulset to 0 to prepare for update (#30450)

When performing a keycloak update, the operator is supposed to make sure that
potential database migrations are run with only one pod active. This change
makes the operator scale down the stateful set to zero pods in preparation for
the update. The next reconciliation loop will scale the stateful set back up
and change the image, making sure migrations are being run on the first pod
that is brought up. This also makes sure that the rollover works even if the
infinispan versions are incompatible. (ref: #30449)

Signed-off-by: Schmidt, Sascha (sasschmidt) <sascha.schmidt@breuninger.de>

issue_title: Event type not set in reset-credential flow under some conditions resulting in an error page
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

We customized our reset-credential flow with a customized authenticator for the first step.
This authenticator can fire some **error** event : context.getEvent().error("My Message");

We noticed that some times those error event trigger an error page and a stacktrace in the logs.
"java.lang.IllegalStateException: Attempted to define event error without first setting the event type"

By looking the stacktrace, we narrow the source to the resetCredentialsGET method of the LoginActionServices class (see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/edde31a1caf8679f042bc35ca9bda92096c0782c/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/LoginActionsService.java#L456)

So if :
1 - the user authentication session is expired in the middle of the reset-credential flow 
2 - the user hit the browser refresh button
3 - the resetPAssword is allowed
then the context's event has **no** event type 
(and the processResetCredential method can fail if an error event is fired)




### Version

22.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The event type must always be set in the reset-credential flow. 
Thus, the calls context.getEvent().error("my message") does not fire an exception.

### Actual behavior

the resetCredentialsGET method of the LoginActionServices class (see https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/edde31a1caf8679f042bc35ca9bda92096c0782c/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/LoginActionsService.java#L456) does not always set the eventype of the EventBuilder instance.

### How to Reproduce?

1 - fire a context.getEvent().error("myMsg") in the "authenticate" method of the first authenticator in the reset-credential flow (ResetCredentialChooseUser.java for instance)

2 - start a rest credential flow, pass the first step

3 - wait (or use the current url of the browser in a private browser)

3 - an ionternal server error is fired

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
This PR intends to always set the event type in order to prevent error when firing an error event.
Closes #30453

issue_title: All user attributes readonly in admin ui and admin API after setting edit mode of one user federation to READ_ONLY
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

We have two ldap user federations connected to different ADs. In only one of those user federations "fed_a" the edit mode is set to READ_ONLY. In another user federation "fed_b" the edit_mode is set to WRITABLE.

Now if we go to the edit view of a user which was imported from "fed_a" in the admin ui the mapped attributes username, email, first name, last name... are all readonly (which is expected).
However, when we then go to the user details of a user from "fed_b" all the mapped attributes are also readonly, which is not expected.
Also, when we then try to add a new user via the admin ui, the mapped attributes are not even displayed and can not be added.
So no user can be added in this state as the username and email fields are also missing.
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/91532004/cd649030-5112-4cc9-b7f1-89642568782c)

Additionally, when we try to add a user via the admin API, the mapped attributes such as email are not set on the user.
These issues only seem to appear if we first go to the edit view of a user from "fed_a".


All these issues do not appear if we change  the edit mode in "fed_a" to WRITABLE or UNSYNCED.

### Version

24.0.5, 25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The user attributes of users that are not imported from the user federation "fed_a" should all be editable at all times.
The add user view in the admin ui should not be affected by the edit mode setting in "fed_a".
The create user request in the admin API should not be affected by the edit mode setting in "fed_a".

### Actual behavior

If we go to the edit view of a user which was imported from "fed_a" in the admin ui the mapped attributes username, email, first name, last name... are all readonly (which is expected).
However, when we then go to the user details of a user from "fed_b" all the mapped attributes are also readonly, which is not expected.
Also, when we then try to add a new user via the admin ui, the mapped attributes are not even displayed and can not be added.
So no user can be added in this state as the username and email fields are also missing.
Additionally, when we try to add a user via the admin API, the mapped attributes such as email are not set on the user.
These issues only seem to appear if we first go to the edit view of a user from "fed_a".

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure two user federations and set one of them to edit mode: READ_ONLY "fed_a"
2. Go to user list and then to user details of a user which was imported from "fed_a"
3. Go to the user details of a user which was not imported from "fed_a"
4. Go to add user in admin UI.
5. Try to add a new keycloak user via admin API 

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Fix LoginFailureEntity protostream encoding
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

The field `lastIPFailure` may be `null` and Google Protocol Buffer specification (v3) does not allow `null` primitive values. If the field is missing, it is set as an empty string. 
We need a Protstream `@ProtoFactory` implementation to convert the empty string into `null` value. 

### Version

main branch

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When reading `LoginFailureEntity` from the Infinispan cache, if it was sent with  `lastIPFailure=null`, it should be created in another Keycloak server with `lastIPFailure=null`.

### Actual behavior

The field is set to an empty string.

### How to Reproduce?

Caught by a test during the development of the new feature: https://github.com/pruivo/keycloak/blob/cfcb73cc78637516d760280dc75a7fc0d3a680b2/testsuite/model/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/model/loginfailure/RemoteLoginFailureTest.java#L150-L175

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix LoginFailureEntity protostream encoding

The field lastIPFailure can be null and needs a proto factory to set it
to null when missing.

Closes #30485

Signed-off-by: Pedro Ruivo <pruivo@redhat.com>

issue_title: Fix AuthenticatedClientSessionEntity protostream encoding
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

Fields `redirectUri` and `action` may be `null` and need attention when unmarshalling. Similar to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/30485

### Version

main

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

If a Keycloak node sends an `AuthenticatedClientSessionEntity` with any of those fields `null`, it should be `null` at the receiver side. 

### Actual behavior

It is set as an empty string as specified by protocol buffers, which does not allow `null` primitives. 

### How to Reproduce?

Caught by a test during the development of the new feature: https://github.com/pruivo/keycloak/blob/cfcb73cc78637516d760280dc75a7fc0d3a680b2/testsuite/model/src/test/java/org/keycloak/testsuite/model/loginfailure/RemoteLoginFailureTest.java#L150-L175



### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Fix AuthenticatedClientSessionEntity protostream encoding

For String fields that may be null, convert an empty string to null when
reading from Protostream

Fixes #30511

issue_title: Flow steps back when changing locale or refreshing page on "Try another way page"
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Authentication flow shows the previous page when user changes locale or refreshes the page on 'try-another-way' (select-authenticator.ftl) page.

### Version

23.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

User stays on the same page

### Actual behavior

User is redirected to the previous page

### How to Reproduce?

1. Configure authentication browser flow with 2 alternative authenticators on the same level
2. User starts this flow
3. User selects one of these authenticators (send OTP by one of the possible ways for example)
4. User clicks on the 'Try another way' link
5. User changes locale or refresh page
6. User is redirected to the previoues page instead of stays on the same page
![5](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/14878466/9fe6bb6c-bfda-4a82-9e56-f40ad7f60d83)



### Anything else?

_No response_
